text:
Once upon a time in a small, picturesque village, there lived an old man named Harold. Harold didn't have much—no thriving business, no vast garden. But he was kind-hearted, fond of nature, and loved to wander along the winding paths that wound through his countryside home. One particularly clear evening, after the birds had put their songs to sleep and the stars had come out to party, Harold set off on a wander.

Deep in the forest, Harold noticed something glinting. He approached eagerly and found a cluster of small, round stones. Each stone gleamed with a different hue—the moon was full tonight!

Inspired, Harold collected them. When he got home, he gathered leaves and made little pots for each stone. The next day, he planted them in the quiet corner of his garden. Over the weeks, each plant grew into a beautiful little flower.

Harold named them 'Moonstone Flowers.' They remained small and delicate, but each bloom seemed to contain a
